trivia: The very first WordPress release, forked from b2/cafelog by Matt Mullenweg and Mike Little. No codename yet — just a hopeful birth announcement on a personal blog.

name: Miles
trivia: Named after jazz great Miles Davis, starting the tradition of jazz-musician codenames. Introduced search-engine-friendly permalinks, the Post Preview pane, and a real plugin API.

name: Mingus
trivia: Introduced the Plugin system as we know it today — the moment WordPress became infinitely extensible.

name: Strayhorn
trivia: Introduced Themes and Pages. The Kubrick theme became a symbol of an era. Downloaded 900,000 times in a single year.

name: Duke
trivia: Shipped a rich WYSIWYG editor (TinyMCE), image uploads, Akismet, and user roles. A New Year's Eve gift from Matt.

name: Ella
trivia: Autosave made its debut. Many blog posts were rescued that day.

name: Getz
trivia: Widgets arrived. Sidebars would never be the same.

name: Dexter
trivia: Native tagging. Before this, people hacked it in with plugins.

name: Brecker
trivia: Wholesale admin redesign by Happy Cog — the first time WordPress looked designed rather than merely assembled.

name: Tyner
trivia: Post revisions. Press This bookmarklet. Word count finally reliable.

name: Coltrane
trivia: New admin UI with left-hand navigation that lasted over a decade. Automatic plugin upgrades from the dashboard.

name: Baker
trivia: Theme installer from the dashboard. Syntax highlighting in the theme editor.

name: Carmen
trivia: Built-in image editor. Trash (finally). Oembed.

name: Thelonious
trivia: Merged WordPress MU — multisite for everyone. Custom post types and custom menus landed. Twenty Ten replaced Kubrick. A civilizational shift.

name: Reinhardt
trivia: Admin Bar arrived, hovering helpfully over your site.

name: Gershwin
trivia: Dropped PHP 4 and MySQL 4 support. Distraction-free writing mode. Twenty Eleven.

name: Sonny
trivia: Drag-and-drop media uploader. Flyout menus. New-user welcome pointers.

name: Green
trivia: Theme Customizer debuted. Twitter embeds.

name: Elvin
trivia: Brand-new Media Manager. Retina-ready admin. Twenty Twelve.

name: Oscar
trivia: Delayed twice. Audio and video improvements. Twenty Thirteen — the most colorful default theme ever.

name: Basie
trivia: Automatic background security updates. Quietly the most important WordPress release of all time.

name: Parker
trivia: Flat, responsive admin (the MP6 project merged in). Admin color schemes. Twenty Fourteen magazine theme.

name: Smith
trivia: Live widget previews. Improved visual editor with drag-and-drop images.

name: Benny
trivia: A symbolic release — the number bump didn't mean a rewrite, but a smoother grid media library and embed previews in the editor.

name: Dinah
trivia: Twenty Fifteen: the most minimal default theme yet. Distraction-free writing mode v2.

name: Powell
trivia: Emoji support everywhere. Press This redesigned. Plugin updates without leaving the page.

name: Billie
trivia: Menus in the Customizer. Site icons from the Customizer. Formatting shortcuts in the editor.

name: Clifford
trivia: REST API infrastructure merged. Responsive images by default. Embed your WordPress posts elsewhere.

name: Coleman
trivia: Inline linking. Custom logos in the Customizer. Formatting keyboard shortcuts.

name: Pepper
trivia: Streamlined updates, native system fonts in the admin, editor content recovery.

name: Vaughan
trivia: REST API content endpoints shipped. Twenty Seventeen. Custom CSS in the Customizer.

name: Evans
trivia: Rich Text Widgets. Audio, Video, Image, HTML widgets. Stage-setting for blocks.

name: Tipton
trivia: Scheduled customization changes. Code-editing safety nets — the last release before Gutenberg lit up the ecosystem.

name: Bebo
trivia: Gutenberg. The block editor. The biggest change to WordPress editing since 2.0's WYSIWYG. Twenty Nineteen as the first block-ready default theme.

name: Betty
trivia: Site Health warnings for outdated PHP. Editor performance improvements.

name: Jaco
trivia: Site Health dashboard. PHP error protection (WSOD). Bumped PHP minimum to 5.6.

name: Kirk
trivia: Twenty Twenty. Accessibility-first admin improvements. Block editor handles long posts.

name: Adderley
trivia: Social Icons block. Buttons block. Released during the first weeks of the pandemic.

name: Eckstine
trivia: Block patterns. Auto-updates for plugins and themes. XML sitemaps in core.

name: Simone
trivia: First major release led by an all-women-identifying release squad. Twenty Twenty-One — a breezy pastel default.

name: Esperanza
trivia: One-click HTTPS migration. Drag-and-drop blocks within columns.

name: Tatum
trivia: Widgets become blocks. Template editor previews Full Site Editing. theme.json arrives quietly.

name: Josephine
trivia: Full Site Editing officially ships. Twenty Twenty-Two — the first block theme. Navigation block.

name: Arturo
trivia: Style variations. Write mode. Locking blocks. FSE matures.

name: Misha
trivia: Fluid typography. Twenty Twenty-Three with 10 style variations.

name: Dolphy
trivia: Site Editor leaves beta. Distraction-free mode returns. Openverse images from the inserter.

name: Lionel
trivia: Command palette. Patterns (renamed from Reusable Blocks). Performance boost — up to 27% faster.

name: Shirley
trivia: Twenty Twenty-Four — the first default theme designed for anyone, for any purpose. Block hooks.

name: Regina
trivia: Font Library. Revisions for styles and templates. Appearance tools for all themes.

name: Dorsey
trivia: Section styles (block style variations). Negative margins. Grid layout experiments.

name: Rollins
trivia: Zoom Out in the editor. Twenty Twenty-Five. Preview themes without activating.

name: Cecil
trivia: Speculative loading in core. Style Book for classic themes. Quiet polish across the editor.

name: Monk
trivia: A landmark release. The collaborative editing foundation lands in core after years in the Phase-3 labs. Twenty Twenty-Six ships a full AI-assisted block styling helper.
